hypothesis: In the REINVENT4 singlet fission task, replacing fixed diversity thresholds with a proportional–integral controller that adjusts similarity-, fragment-, and n-gram-penalty weights each step to keep the batch mean pairwise Tanimoto similarity in [0.11, 0.13] will preserve ≥95% fragment uniqueness and achieve a final average property score ≥95% of the baseline (no-constraint) run, thereby eliminating the optimization slowdown observed with fixed combined constraints.
